sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the pattern more clearly. With the middle VERTICAL bar OFF:
- Frame 0: (13, 30)
- Frame 1: (17, 30) - down
- Frame 2: (17, 30) - hold
- Frame 3: (21, 30) - down
- Frame 4: (21, 34) - RIGHT 4 (cols shifted from 30-33 to 34-37)
- Frame 5: (21, 30) - back to center
- Frame 6: reset to (13, 30)
So vertical bars control RIGHT movement (opposite of horizontal bars which control LEFT)!
Let me summarize what I've learned:
- Horizontal bars (when OFF): cause LEFT movement at different steps
  - Left horizontal OFF: left at step 1 (after first down)
  - Middle horizontal OFF: left at step 3 (after second down)
  - Right horizontal OFF: left at step 5 (after hold)
- Vertical bars (when OFF): cause RIGHT movement at different steps
  - Middle vertical OFF: right at step 4
So the 6 buttons control 6 possible lateral moves (3 left, 3 right) at different points in the falling sequence.
The U-shape container is at cols 29-34. The yellow object starts at cols 30-33. The object is 4 columns wide (30, 31, 32, 33). The U-shape opening is at row 33, cols 29-34.
For the object to fit into the U-shape, it needs to be at the right horizontal position when it reaches the container's depth. The container interior seems to be at cols 30-33 (inside the walls at 29 and 34).
The default animation (all buttons ON) makes the object fall straight down. Let me check what happens with ALL buttons ON - does the object fall into the container?
Actually, let me reconsider. The animation always resets. So maybe the goal is NOT about the object physically falling into the container. Maybe the goal is to create a specific pattern of button toggles that matches some target.
Let me look at the U-shape more carefully. The U-shape is at rows 33-37, cols 29-34. It has:
- Left wall: col 29, rows 33-37
- Right wall: col 34, rows 33-37
- Bottom: row 37, cols 29-34
- Opening at top (row 33)
The yellow object is a T-shape at rows 13-16, cols 30-33.
Maybe the goal is to make the yellow object land exactly inside the U-shape. For that, I need to figure out what button configuration makes the object fall straight down (no lateral movement) and land at the right depth.
Wait, let me re-examine. With all buttons ON (default), what happens? Let me reset all buttons and test.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Reset middle vertical bar back ON
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 45, 'col': 36}])

# Now test with ALL buttons ON (default state)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 36}])

if last_animation:
    for entry in last_animation:
        for j, frame in enumerate(entry.frames):
            seg = frame.segmentation
            y_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y']
            print(f"Frame {j}:")
            for n in y_nodes:
                print(f"  Yellow: boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
